textPlain: Very nice and satisfying, but I would suggest ramping up the difficulty a little faster. I got until lvl 15 by just flipping the obvious areas, taking no more than 2 seconds. It's otherwise very cool and minimalist.Edit: limiting it to square flips was a great idea. There are just enough moves to make the answer non-obvious (after lvl 15), but not so many possible moves that you get overwhelmed.---Edit 2: I just remembered I made a similar "game"[1], where you select columns to XOR with other columns and try to reach the target pattern. Use the scroll wheel and shift+wheel to change the pattern and size.That was actually part of a real research project in optimizing circuits for computing binary finite fields, where the "game" was a sandbox to try different algorithms. The best algorithm was actually found by someone playing in this sandbox and coming up with an efficient strategy.[1] https://boppreh.com/source/playreduce/
Really nice concept and super clean execution. Wish I’d had 1/2 this restraint on adding complexity when I made my own tile-based puzzle game[0][0] https://www.hidden-mirrors.com
Did I just get flipped in level 13? I really like it, the level progression feels good. Very nice execution.
Played until 27. All the levels were pretty easy, 15-30 seconds per level. Mainly because of the "par" feature
Nice job. I'm reminded of the "Lights Out" game [1].https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lights_Out_(game)
I like it. Played a bunch of levels. I could feel my brain learning new patterns (like the card game Set). There are a handful of really useful primitives.
Stopped at 74 but managed to par all before that somehow. Didn't really do any problem solving/deep thinking about it, just clicking what felt right
Love it. The “par” value is a huge hint. You might consider hiding it
Android gestures need to be prevented in order for the game to work on mobile (drag finger from right to left go back). Nice game thought.
Level 24. I took a natural approach (pull each corner to the center) and got a rather unfortunate picture—-Edit: and the right answer was so obvious, too
I really love how the first few levels teach you about various solving elements and patterns. very well done.
Neumorphic knot, a puzzle about Knot patterns: https://brainteaser.top/knot/index.html
Had a lot of fun with this, great job on the concept and presentation!
I agree the ramp up is a bit slow, but I love the concept, and the interface. I am stuck at level 26edit: found a way to solve it in 6, but not in 4
That makes sense. I think what I'm gonna try is hiding it until the level is solved at least once.
Interesting. I don't get that on my android phone. What model and browser are you using?
Yes I got stuck there for a while. I did a few more after that but my interest waned. I did spend a lot fo time feeling ike a pixel in Conway's game of Life.
